Off Calendar Feb-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. As to breach of contract and the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, no integration clause exists and the parties dispute the meaning of contractual terms; these matters are to be resolved by litigation. A cause of action for intentional interference is sufficiently pled; the interference alleged is more than speculative. The 17200 claim is adequately pled. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Richard B. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff'S Verified Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's Verified Complaint is DENIED. As to punitive damages, the facts pleaded are not "wholly conclusory." The prayer for restitution is not limited to the Business & Professions 17200 claim and "restitution" is an ambiguous term. Whether CCP 1021.5 applies often cannot be determined until a case is litigated. Defendant may deny as irrelevant "improper and argumentative passages" in the complaint. ORDER SIGNED. RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: PLAINTIFF SUSAN CHAN's Motion For Attorney's Fees is denied without prejudice as premature. See Roberts v Packard, Packard & Johnson, -- Cal. Rptr.--, 2013 WL 3357120 (Cal. App. July 3, 2013); Frog Creek Partners, LLC v. Vance Brown, Inc., 206 Cal. App. 4th 515, 535 (2012). MOTION ALSO DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1021.5. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
